title: Translation review checklist plus back-translation QA procedure
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual translates reviewed oncology glossary entries into under-served languages. A translation review checklist is required so qualified medical-translation reviewers can verify accuracy, meaning preservation (especially negations and urgency), cultural appropriateness, and termbase adherence. Back-translation QA is mandatory for all terms with safetyLoad other than none.
objective: Author a translation review checklist and back-translation QA procedure for qualified medical-translation reviewers, covering: meaning preservation, negation/urgency/consent accuracy, termbase adherence, cultural appropriateness, notMedicalAdvice notice in target language, and mandatory back-translation QA steps for safety-load terms.
acceptanceCriteria: templates/translation-review-checklist.md is created with checkable items covering: meaning preservation (especially negations, urgency, consent terms), termbase adherence, cultural appropriateness, notMedicalAdvice notice in target language, UTF-8 encoding, and agentFlag resolution
Procedure specifies that back-translation QA is mandatory for every term with safetyLoad other than none, with comparison to the reviewed source definition before sign-off
Checklist includes a sign-off block capturing: reviewer name, language pair, medical-translation qualification, COI declaration, date, and QA results for back-translated terms
Procedure references clinical-review-checklist.md (review-001) for the source-side accuracy requirements
resources: PLAN.md — Quality, review and risk gates section
PLAN.md — Artifacts / data model section (safetyLoad enum)
output: templates/translation-review-checklist.md including the back-translation QA procedure
